To combat grid congestion, the Netherlands must invest tens of billions. How did it get to this point? metadata.source_title: Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

text: The Dutch power grid is creaking at the seams. To combat grid congestion, the Netherlands must invest tens of billions. How did it get to this point? Journalist Esther van Rijswijk discusses this in this episode of the podcast series Achter de Cijfers with sector economist Albert Jan Swart. Electric cooking, driving and heating ensure an increase in our electricity consumption, while solar panels and wind turbines provide more and more energy.

metadata.source_title: Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

text: How did it get to this point? Journalist Esther van Rijswijk discusses this in this episode of the podcast series Achter de Cijfers with sector economist Albert Jan Swart. Electric cooking, driving and heating ensure an increase in our electricity consumption, while solar panels and wind turbines provide more and more energy. The result? Peak moments when demand and supply are too high at the same time.

metadata.source_title: Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

text: Journalist Esther van Rijswijk discusses this in this episode of the podcast series Achter de Cijfers with sector economist Albert Jan Swart. Electric cooking, driving and heating ensure an increase in our electricity consumption, while solar panels and wind turbines provide more and more energy. The result? Peak moments when demand and supply are too high at the same time. This phenomenon is called grid congestion and now leads to new homes or businesses no longer being connected.

metadata.source_title: Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

text: Electric cooking, driving and heating ensure an increase in our electricity consumption, while solar panels and wind turbines provide more and more energy. The result? Peak moments when demand and supply are too high at the same time. This phenomenon is called grid congestion and now leads to new homes or businesses no longer being connected. Albert Jan Swart, sector economist at ABN AMRO, talks in this podcast about what lies behind the problems on our electricity grid.

metadata.source_title: Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

text: The result? Peak moments when demand and supply are too high at the same time. This phenomenon is called grid congestion and now leads to new homes or businesses no longer being connected. Albert Jan Swart, sector economist at ABN AMRO, talks in this podcast about what lies behind the problems on our electricity grid. From the historical structure of our energy network to the billions of investments that are now required - and the role that our own behavior can play.

A number of significant steps have been taken in the past year to reduce the consequences of grid congestion.

metadata.source_title: Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

text: Because maybe moving some laundry or loading your car is more important than you think. What to do about grid congestion? More information. A number of significant steps have been taken in the past year to reduce the consequences of grid congestion. For example, the new Energy Act has been adopted and the ACM has already adjusted many policy rules to give companies more room to use the scarce capacity on the grid in a smarter way.

metadata.source_title: Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

text: What to do about grid congestion? More information. A number of significant steps have been taken in the past year to reduce the consequences of grid congestion. For example, the new Energy Act has been adopted and the ACM has already adjusted many policy rules to give companies more room to use the scarce capacity on the grid in a smarter way. In the report [Grid Congestion: What's New](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/alle-sectoren/netcongestie-voor-wat-hoort-wat.html), ABN AMRO explains what entrepreneurs can do to quickly use more electricity despite grid congestion.
